sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: DOWN.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 12, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Agent now at left passage (boundary [[14,20],[14,24],[18,24],[18,20]]). History shows LEFT+DOWN works from [10,22]→[10,16]→left passage. From left passage, RIGHT blocked. Need to check if DOWN works from left passage.
- Goal model: Navigate to end zone.
- Action model: Agent moves 6 cells. From [10,16], DOWN moves to left passage. From left passage, need to find next move.
- Recent findings: Agent stuck at [10,16]. Earlier RIGHT and DOWN worked, but now blocked. Maze structure may have changed.
- Open questions: Does the maze scroll or does the agent move through it? What's the actual path?
- Plan: Try DOWN from left passage. If blocked, need to find alternate route through right passage.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 11 of about 78 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Great! DOWN from the left passage worked! The agent moved to a new position with boundary [[14, 32], [14, 38], [38, 38], [38, 20], [26, 20], [26, 31], [25, 32]]. This is the large white passage area (id=10), which includes:
- Rows 14-25: cols 32-38 (right passage top)
- Rows 26-38: cols 20-38 (wide passage)
So the agent moved from the left passage (rows 14-18, cols 20-24) to the right/wide passage (rows 14-38, cols 20-38). This is a big jump!
The boundary [[14, 32], [14, 38], [38, 38], [38, 20], [26, 20], [26, 31], [25, 32]] describes the white passage area. The agent is now inside this area.
Let me trace the boundary:
- (14, 32) to (14, 38): top edge at row 14, cols 32-38
- (14, 38) to (38, 38): right edge at col 38, rows 14-38
- (38, 38) to (38, 20): bottom edge at row 38, cols 20-38
- (38, 20) to (26, 20): left edge at col 20, rows 26-38
- (26, 20) to (26, 31): top edge at row 26, cols 20-31
- (26, 31) to (25, 32): corner
- (25, 32) to (14, 32): left edge at col 32, rows 14-25
So the white passage area is:
- Rows 14-25: cols 32-38 (right passage top)
- Rows 26-38: cols 20-38 (wide passage)
The agent is now inside this area. The agent's position is the center of this area, which is approximately [26, 29] (the centroid).
From here, the agent can:
- DOWN: Move to the bottom passage (rows 42-43, cols 20-63)
- RIGHT: Move to the end zone (rows 49-55, cols 43-49)
- LEFT: Move back to the left passage
- UP: Move back to the left passage
Let me try DOWN to move to the bottom passage.
Actually, looking at the image, the agent (blue square) is now at the bottom-left of the maze, near the red path. The end zone is at the bottom-right. The agent needs to move RIGHT to reach the end zone.
But wait, the agent is inside the white passage area (rows 14-38, cols 20-38). The end zone is at rows 49-55, cols 43-49, which is outside this area. So the agent needs to move DOWN to reach the bottom passage, then RIGHT to reach the end zone.
Let me try DOWN.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Agent moved DOWN from left passage to the large white passage area (rows 14-38, cols 20-38). This is progress! The agent is now in the right/wide passage.
Goal model: Navigate to end zone at bottom-right.
Action model: DOWN from left passage moves to right/wide passage. Need to continue DOWN to bottom passage, then RIGHT to end zone.
Plan: Try DOWN to move to bottom passage, then RIGHT to end zone.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try DOWN from current position
action(['DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
agent = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id']==8][0]
print(f"After DOWN: agent={agent['boundary']}")
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")

# Check all nodes to see agent position relative to end zone
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] in [6, 7, 8]:  # start, red path, agent
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
